channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Taber, Alberta receives funding for arena reconstruction and community fundraising efforts
item.description: Taber, a southern Alberta town east of Lethbridge, has been awarded $50,000 as one of the provincial winners of the Kraft Hockeyville contest. This comes after an explosion in December last year rende...

item.title: Rising Oil Prices Could Weigh Heavily on Pakistan’s Economy and Current Account
item.description: Karachi is facing a potential economic storm as oil prices soar above $100 due to the ongoing Middle East conflict. Former finance minister Hafiz Pasha warns that Pakistan’s GDP could shrink by 1.0 to...

item.title: Discrepancy Between Oil Futures and Physical Oil Prices Amid Middle East Tensions
item.description: The article discusses the current divergence between oil futures traded on the market and the prices of actual physical crude oil. While futures, sometimes called 'paper oil,' reflect expectations tha...

item.title: Live Updates on U.S. and Israeli Military Operations Against Iran
item.description: The live coverage tracks the escalating conflict between the United States, Israel, and Iran that began on February 28, 2026, with surprise aerial bombardments on multiple Iranian cities amid ongoing ...

item.title: Study Finds Prostate Cancer Screening Comparable to Breast Cancer Checks
item.description: A recent study indicates that screening for prostate cancer could be as effective as routine mammograms for breast cancer. The research, conducted at the German Cancer Research Centre and presented at...

item.title: Court Allows Al-Quds Rally in Toronto; Police Make Two Arrests During Event
item.description: On Saturday, downtown Toronto saw a heavy police presence during the Al-Quds rally after an Ontario judge denied the provincial government's request for an injunction to stop the event. The court cite...

item.title: Iran Reports Missile and Drone Attacks on U.S. Naval Assets in the Middle East
item.description: Iran has claimed responsibility for attacks on the U.S. aircraft carrier Abraham Lincoln and other vessels in the Fifth Fleet with missiles and drones, according to the military spokesperson Khatam Al...

item.title: University of Maryland Develops Wearable Device to Measure Human Flatulence and Gut Activity
item.description: Researchers at the University of Maryland have introduced a wearable device called Smart Underwear, designed to objectively monitor human flatulence by detecting hydrogen gas produced by gut microbes....

item.title: Kerosene price jumps by Rs40 per litre while petrol and diesel prices stay steady
item.description: The government of Pakistan has once again increased the price of kerosene oil by Rs40 per litre, making it the most expensive fuel for consumers at Rs358 per litre. This latest hike adds to the substa...

item.title: Former KDF Soldier Explains Reasons for Leaving Military and Challenges After Service
item.description: A former Kenya Defence Forces (KDF) soldier, Stanley Gichira, has shared his experience of leaving the military voluntarily, citing concerns about health and unfair treatment. He explained that witnes...

item.title: US-Iran Conflict Escalates as Gulf States Targeted and Kharg Island Hit
item.description: Tensions in the Middle East have intensified as Iran launched a widespread missile and drone campaign against Gulf countries, surprising the United States and its allies. US President Donald Trump exp...

item.title: Several Iranian women's football players return home despite asylum offers in Australia
item.description: Several members of Iran's women's national football team, who were in Australia for the Asian Cup and had been granted humanitarian visas, have chosen to return to Iran. Initially, seven players recei...

item.title: West Asia Conflict Impacts India’s Domestic Security Measures
item.description: Former West Bengal Governor Gopalkrishna Gandhi has remarked that the ongoing conflict in West Asia is no longer distant and has tangible effects on India’s domestic life. Speaking at a panel discussi...

item.title: China's legislation promotes Putonghua at the expense of minority languages
item.description: China has recently passed a law that mandates the use of Putonghua, the national common language, across all educational and official settings, putting minority languages under increasing pressure. Hi...

item.title: Changan Deepal S07 L: A Glamorous Electric SUV with Comfort and Style
item.description: The Changan Deepal S07 L, a new all-electric vehicle from the Chinese automaker Changan, has arrived in South Africa with a focus on style and technology rather than pure practicality. Priced at R995,...

item.title: Tinubu Highlights Need for Collective Effort in Nigeria’s Development
item.description: For real, the President, Bola Tinubu, don yarn how Nigeria no fit rely only on the federal government for her development. Na during one media chat he talk say make all Nigerians, private sector, stat...

item.title: Warwick Davis reveals new partner Yvette, nicknamed 'Sponge', a year after wife's death
item.description: Actor Warwick Davis, best known for his roles in Harry Potter and Willow, has been dating a woman named Yvette for over a year following the tragic death of his wife, Samantha Davis, in March 2024. Sa...

item.title: Two men arrested in connection with the killing of three gold buyers in Cagayan
item.description: MANILA, Philippines — Police authorities have arrested two suspects in relation to the murder of three gold buyers in Lal-lo, Cagayan. Frederick Usana, 31, and Rodel Sabal, 48, were taken into custody...

item.title: Court of Appeal Suspends High Court Ruling on President's 21 Advisers
item.description: The Court of Appeal has granted President William Ruto and his 21 presidential advisers a temporary reprieve by suspending a High Court ruling that declared their positions unconstitutional. This deci...

item.title: Quebec's Local Whisky Sees Strong Sales Growth Following U.S. Import Ban
item.description: Quebec’s whisky industry has experienced a significant boost in sales since the removal of U.S. alcoholic products from SAQ shelves, following tariffs imposed by former U.S. President Donald Trump. Th...
